exam.name: Praxis Core Academic Skills for Educators: Mathematics
exam.category_description: Exam-aligned prep for future educators with syllabus-mapped coverage built directly from official certification blueprints.
exam.hero_headline: Pass Praxis Core Math with exam-accurate coverage
exam.hero_subheadline: Master every tested objective in 15-minute topics mapped to the official ETS study companion.
exam.companion_problem: Most Praxis prep is either too shallow or too broad, making it hard to know what is actually test-relevant.
exam.companion_solution: We convert the official syllabus into a complete, topic-sized learning map with retention-focused notes and consistent concept coverage.

sections.title: Number and Quantity
sections.description: Understand integers, decimals, fractions, ratios, percent, constant rates, place value, and whole number properties.

topics.title: Integers, Decimals, and Fractions
topics.description: Review core arithmetic operations involving integers, decimals, and fractions.
learning_objectives: Perform basic operations on integers, decimals, and fractions.
Understand the rules for adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing these number types.

topics.title: Ratios and Proportions
topics.description: Understand and apply ratio and proportion concepts to solve problems.
learning_objectives: Set up proportions to solve for an unknown value.
Use ratio language to describe relationships between two quantities.

topics.title: Percentages and Percent Change
topics.description: Compute percentages, find percent of a number, and calculate percent change.
learning_objectives: Convert between fractions, decimals, and percents.
Calculate the percent increase or decrease of a given value.

topics.title: Constant Rates and Rates of Change
topics.description: Solve problems involving constant rates like miles per hour or gallons per minute.
learning_objectives: Calculate unit rates.
Use rates to determine total distance, time, or cost.

topics.title: Place Value, Naming, and Ordering Numbers
topics.description: Understand place value systems, name decimals, and order numbers on a number line.
learning_objectives: Identify the place value of digits in whole numbers and decimals.
Compare and order a mix of positive and negative fractions and decimals.

topics.title: Properties of Whole Numbers: Factors and Multiples
topics.description: Work with factors, multiples, even and odd numbers, and understand divisibility.
learning_objectives: Identify factors and multiples of given whole numbers.
Use the rules of evens and odds in basic operations.

topics.title: Prime Numbers and Divisibility Rules
topics.description: Identify prime and composite numbers and utilize divisibility rules.
learning_objectives: Find the prime factorization of a number.
Determine if a number is divisible by 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9, or 10.
Find the GCD and LCM of two numbers.

topics.title: Identifying Arithmetic Counterexamples
topics.description: Evaluate mathematical statements and identify counterexamples.
learning_objectives: Understand the definition of a counterexample.
Find specific numerical examples that disprove a general arithmetic claim.

topics.title: Real-Life Word Problems and Rounding
topics.description: Interpret real-world scenarios to extract mathematical operations and round answers appropriately.
learning_objectives: Translate word problems into mathematical operations.
Apply rounding rules to find approximate answers to nearest specified place value.

topics.title: Units of Measurement and Conversions
topics.description: Solve problems involving U.S. customary and metric units, including conversions.
learning_objectives: Convert within the metric system.
Convert within the U.S. customary system.
Use dimensional analysis for unit conversions.

topics.title: Applying Basic Number Properties
topics.description: Recognize and use commutative, associative, and distributive properties.
learning_objectives: Apply basic number properties to simplify arithmetic operations without necessarily naming the properties.
Understand how operations are grouped.

topics.title: Exponents and Roots Approximations
topics.description: Calculate with integer exponents and find approximations for roots like pi or sqrt(2).
learning_objectives: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide numbers with integer exponents.
Estimate the value of irrational numbers like pi, sqrt(2), and sqrt(5).

sections.title: Data Interpretation and Representation, Statistics, and Probability
sections.description: Read and interpret visual displays of data, make inferences, determine measures of central tendency and spread, and assign probabilities.

topics.title: Reading and Interpreting Data Tables and Charts
topics.description: Extract and interpret information from data tables and frequency charts.
learning_objectives: Read data from tables.
Use frequency charts to determine totals and relationships.

topics.title: Reading and Interpreting Bar and Line Graphs
topics.description: Understand visual displays of quantitative data using bar graphs and line graphs.
learning_objectives: Compare quantities using bar graphs.
Analyze trends over time using line graphs.

topics.title: Reading and Interpreting Circle Graphs
topics.description: Interpret circle graphs (pie charts) to understand part-to-whole data relationships.
learning_objectives: Calculate specific category amounts given a total and a circle graph.
Convert percentages in a circle graph to numerical data.

topics.title: Stem-and-Leaf Plots and Boxplots
topics.description: Analyze data distributions through stem-and-leaf plots and boxplots.
learning_objectives: Read a stem-and-leaf plot to find specific data points.
Interpret the median and quartiles from a boxplot.

topics.title: Measures of Central Tendency: Mean, Median, Mode
topics.description: Compute and interpret the mean, median, and mode of a given data set.
learning_objectives: Calculate the arithmetic mean, median, and mode.
Understand how adding or multiplying constants to data affects these measures.

topics.title: Measures of Spread: Range and Standard Deviation
topics.description: Determine the range and understand the standard deviation of data sets.
learning_objectives: Calculate the range of a data set.
Interpret what a high or low standard deviation means about the spread of data.

topics.title: Random Sampling and Population Inferences
topics.description: Use data from random samples to draw conclusions about entire populations.
learning_objectives: Understand the concept of random sampling.
Make reasonable inferences and predictions about a population based on sample proportions.

topics.title: Scatterplots and Linear Relationships
topics.description: Identify and describe positive and negative linear relationships in scatterplots.
learning_objectives: Plot points on a scatterplot.
Determine the direction (positive, negative, or none) of the relationship between two variables.

topics.title: Linear Models and Data Predictions
topics.description: Use lines of best fit to make predictions for a given data set.
learning_objectives: Interpret the equation of a line of best fit in context.
Use a linear model to predict the value of a dependent variable.

topics.title: Correlation vs. Causation
topics.description: Distinguish between two variables being correlated and one causing the other.
learning_objectives: Explain the difference between correlation and causation.
Evaluate contextual scenarios to determine if causation can be justified.

topics.title: Outliers in Data Sets
topics.description: Identify outliers and understand their effect on statistical measures.
learning_objectives: Recognize outliers in a data set or visual display.
Describe how an outlier impacts the mean versus the median.

topics.title: Basic Concepts of Probability
topics.description: Compute the probability of simple events occurring.
learning_objectives: Define probability as the ratio of favorable outcomes to total possible outcomes.
Calculate the probability of a simple event (e.g., rolling a die).

topics.title: Probability of Independent Events
topics.description: Calculate the probability of two independent events occurring together.
learning_objectives: Understand what makes events independent.
Multiply the probabilities of individual events to find the joint probability.

topics.title: Counting Techniques and Combinations
topics.description: Solve problems using basic counting techniques to determine total possible outcomes.
learning_objectives: Use counting techniques to find total outcomes.
Evaluate combinations of events, like tossing a pair of number cubes.

sections.title: Algebra and Geometry
sections.description: Manipulate algebraic expressions, solve word problems and equations, and understand properties of geometric shapes.

topics.title: Properties of Basic Operations
topics.description: Understand properties like commutative, associative, and distributive laws without needing to know their names.
learning_objectives: Apply basic operational properties to simplify algebraic expressions.
Recognize that multiplication is repeated addition.

topics.title: Following Arithmetic and Algebraic Procedures
topics.description: Follow step-by-step procedures, simple flowcharts, or recurrence sequences.
learning_objectives: Execute an algebraic procedure based on given steps.
Analyze the outcome of following an algorithmic flowchart.

topics.title: Equivalent Algebraic Expressions
topics.description: Use operations properties to identify or generate equivalent expressions.
learning_objectives: Simplify expressions by combining like terms.
Expand expressions using the distributive property to find equivalent forms.

topics.title: Writing Algebraic Equations and Expressions
topics.description: Translate verbal descriptions and real-life mathematical problems into algebraic notation.
learning_objectives: Write an algebraic expression from a verbal description.
Formulate an equation that models a contextualized real-world problem.

topics.title: Solving Linear Equations in One Variable
topics.description: Solve linear equations algebraically for a single variable.
learning_objectives: Isolate the variable to solve single-step and multi-step linear equations.
Check the solution by plugging it back into the equation.

topics.title: Solving Simple Quadratic Equations
topics.description: Solve straightforward quadratic equations algebraically.
learning_objectives: Solve quadratic equations of the form x^2 = c.
Understand that equations like x^2 = 49 have both positive and negative roots.

topics.title: Linear Word Problems and Applications
topics.description: Solve practical word problems involving linear relationships.
learning_objectives: Identify the unknown variable in a word problem.
Construct and solve a linear equation based on the constraints of the problem.

topics.title: Using Venn Diagrams for Word Problems
topics.description: Solve word problems using sets and Venn diagrams.
learning_objectives: Organize overlapping information into a Venn diagram.
Compute the intersection, union, or missing parts of sets based on context.

topics.title: The Coordinate Plane and Linear Graphs
topics.description: Understand the xy-coordinate plane and plot linear equations.
learning_objectives: Plot points and lines on a Cartesian coordinate system.
Understand how linear equations represent lines on a graph.

topics.title: Understanding Slope and Intercepts
topics.description: Determine the slope and intercepts of a line from a graph, table, or equation.
learning_objectives: Calculate the slope of a line from two points.
Identify the x-intercept and y-intercept of a given linear equation.

topics.title: Equations of Lines
topics.description: Write equations of lines in point-slope and slope-intercept form.
learning_objectives: Convert between point-slope and slope-intercept forms of a line.
Write the equation of a line given its slope and a point on it.

topics.title: Properties of Angles and Intersecting Lines
topics.description: Utilize facts about angles formed by intersecting and parallel lines.
learning_objectives: Determine measures of vertical, complementary, and supplementary angles.
Find congruent angles when parallel lines are cut by a transversal.

topics.title: Triangles: Types, Angles, and Inequality Property
topics.description: Classify triangles and use triangle properties to solve problems.
learning_objectives: Classify triangles by sides (scalene, isosceles, equilateral) and angles (acute, right, obtuse).
Apply the triangle inequality theorem to determine valid side lengths.

topics.title: Congruence and Similarity of Geometric Figures
topics.description: Utilize facts about congruent and similar shapes.
learning_objectives: Identify congruent triangles to find missing side lengths or angles.
Use scale factors and proportional reasoning to solve problems with similar figures.

topics.title: Properties of Quadrilaterals
topics.description: Identify and classify different types of quadrilaterals based on their features.
learning_objectives: Distinguish between parallelograms, rectangles, squares, rhombuses, and trapezoids.
Use properties of quadrilaterals to find missing measurements.

topics.title: Perimeter and Area of Triangles and Rectangles
topics.description: Use formulas for perimeter and area of basic polygons.
learning_objectives: Calculate the perimeter and area of a rectangle or square.
Apply the formula (1/2)*base*height to find the area of a triangle.

topics.title: Circles: Circumference, Area, and Angles
topics.description: Solve problems involving properties of circles.
learning_objectives: Use formulas to calculate the circumference and area of a circle.
Differentiate between radius and diameter, and understand central vs. inscribed angles.

topics.title: Volume of Rectangular Prisms
topics.description: Compute the volume of three-dimensional rectangular solids.
learning_objectives: Apply the volume formula (length × width × height) for a rectangular box.
Solve word problems involving the volume of rectangular prisms.
